description:
Query the Unity asset reference graph database to search for asset relationships in both directions.

Supports two search directions:
- 'references': Find which assets reference (use) the given asset (incoming edges). Answers: "what uses this asset?"
- 'dependencies': Find which assets the given asset depends on (outgoing edges). Answers: "what does this asset use?"

Given an asset path (relative to the Unity project root, e.g. 'Assets/Scenes/Main.unity'), this tool resolves its GUID and queries the reference graph. The reference graph must have been built via a prior scan.

Optionally supports depth expansion (max_depth > 1) to find transitive references or dependencies.
Optionally supports type_filter to narrow results to specific asset types (e.g. 'script', 'prefab|scene').

Returns results grouped by asset path with detailed ref_path showing the exact location within the asset where the reference occurs, including the GameObject hierarchy path, component type, and field name (e.g. 'Root/Child/MonoBehaviour/m_Script').
- When Headroom is enabled and output exceeds the compress threshold, large results are compressed through headroom-ai (requires headroom proxy or HEADROOM_API_KEY).

asset_path.description: Path to the asset file relative to the Unity project root (e.g. 'Assets/Prefabs/Player.prefab')

direction.description: Search direction. 'references' finds assets that reference this asset (incoming edges). 'dependencies' finds assets this asset depends on (outgoing edges).

max_depth.description: Maximum traversal depth. 1 = direct only (default). Values > 1 enable transitive search.

type_filter.description: Filter results by asset type. Supports '|' separated multiple types (OR logic). Valid types: scene, prefab, material, animation, controller, genericAsset, script, texture, audio, shader, model, otherYaml, metaOnly. Example: 'script|prefab' returns only scripts and prefabs.
